items.summary: Katherine couple Nicki and Luke Asling have been living on their back verandah for the past three months, after their home was flooded to the ceiling in March. Local renovators say it will take months for all the repairs in town to be completed.

items.title: Fremantle Dockers embrace premiership favouritism as club flexes muscle
items.summary: Hard-bitten Dockers supporters won't want to say it out loud, but as Fremantle flexes more muscle with each passing week, hope is slowly turning to expectation that a first AFL flag could be on the cards.

items.title: How Socceroo Nestory Irankunda got his start with Adelaide's Northern Wolves
items.summary: Long before Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal buoyed Australian fans he made his start with Adelaide's Northern Wolves Soccer Club, where he is a hero to many other young players who share a similar background.

items.title: Northern hairy-nosed wombats are 'picky' with their homes, but they may survive in other habitats
items.summary: One of the world's most critically endangered animals may be able to live in more areas than conservationists realised.

items.title: Injured toddler's ordeal highlights problem of weather radar blackspot
items.summary: When two-year-old Teddy suffered a suspected broken leg on his family's outback property, the journey to hospital was complicated by a lack of reliable weather forecasting.

items.title: Tasmania Devils AFL coaching choice is a blessing and a curse

items.title: Energy industry sector weighs in on AI data centre risks to Marinus Link

items.title: Don't expect a post-budget $1,250 boost this tax time
items.summary: There was a lot of talk about the $1,000 instant tax reduction and the $250 Working Australians Tax Offset in the federal budget. But they won't be in play at tax time this year.

items.title: Franchisees ruined after 7-Eleven forces, then blocks sale of petrol station

items.title: KPMG the latest example of accountants being unaccountable

items.title: Pauline Hanson's criticism of Guardian reporter labelled an 'assault on free press'

items.title: Pauline Hanson wasn't there for the National Press Club. She was there for Fight Club
items.summary: The spectacle of the One Nation leader having a blue with journalists — or indeed anyone even vaguely associated with Canberra incumbency — is catnip for the Hanson algorithm. And the lady is not stupid.

items.summary: In an ASX announcement on Wednesday, ARN Media agreed to pay the shock jock $12.09 million, with $3 million payable as soon as next month.

items.title: Queensland government accused of removing Aboriginal people from health roles in 'Project Invisibility'
